steps.title: Identify the cautionary framing
steps.content: The opening directly warns against overstating posture's cognitive effects. Then “A case in point:” introduces the O'Brien and Ahmed study — this phrase marks the study as an example supporting that warning.

steps.title: Match the purpose to the choices
steps.content:
D identifies the passage's purpose: using the study to illustrate why caution is needed about posture-cognition claims.

The null result (no effect of posture on risk decisions) directly supports the opening warning against overstatement.

The other choices either mischaracterize what the passage does (critique prior research, or show misunderstood findings) or misframe the study's role (as solving a problem rather than supporting a principle).

That is the correct answer.
